build-the-quick-start-scene.caption: Project workflow shown in the SceneMax IDE.

quick-start.caption: Dragon Hello World script used in the tutorial.
quick-start.code:
sys.print "Hello World!"
skybox.show solar system
d is a dragon; d.fly loop

printing-text.caption: Print, style, update, and clear text through a named channel.
printing-text.code:
sys.print "Hello World": pos (100,200,0), color yellow, size 3, font "yoshi"
wait 3 seconds
sys.print ""

playing-3d-animations.caption: SceneMax example from animation.md.
playing-3d-animations.code:
s is a sinbad
s.Dance
s.Dance at speed of 2

chaining-animations.caption: SceneMax example from animation.md.
chaining-animations.code:
player=>Ninja
player.Kick at speed of 0.5 
  then Backflip 
  then "Spin" at speed of 2 
  then Block 
  then SideKick at speed of 0.1

defining-arrays.caption: SceneMax example for Defining Arrays.
defining-arrays.code:
var objects = ["Sinbad", "Ninja", "Fighter1", "helicopter", "house"]
my_object => (objects[rnd(5)])
my_object.move left 5 in 3 seconds

object-arrays.caption: SceneMax example for Object Arrays.
object-arrays.code:
s1 => sinbad
s2 => ninja
s3 => bird sprite
b => box

var my_objects = [s1,s2,s3,b]
var random_obj = my_objects[rnd(4)]
random_obj.turn left 360 in 10 seconds

nested-arrays.caption: SceneMax example for Nested Arrays.
nested-arrays.code:
var easy_wave = ["Ninja", "Ninja", "Sinbad"]
var hard_wave = ["Fighter1", "Ninja", "helicopter"]
var waves = [easy_wave, hard_wave]
var current_wave = waves[0]

project-and-file-operations-what-you-can.caption: Fighting-game-style example for Project and File Operations.
project-and-file-operations-what-you-can.code:
// AI-assisted workflow result: inspect, edit, then preview the current scene
run set_camera_on_player
run shake_the_world async
header.print "MCP-assisted scene update ready"

scene-designer-operations-what-you-can-a.caption: Fighting-game-style example for Scene Designer Operations.

claude-code-setup.caption: Fighting-game-style example for Claude Code Setup.
claude-code-setup.code:
// AI-assisted workflow result: inspect, edit, then preview the current scene
run set_camera_on_player
run shake_the_world async
header.print "MCP-assisted scene update ready"

physics-motion-commands.caption: SceneMax example from collisions-and-physics.md.
physics-motion-commands.code:
rock => meshy_rock1 : mass 8, collision shape box
enemy => fighter2 : pos (12, 0, 4)

collision-detection.caption: SceneMax example from collisions-and-physics.md.
collision-detection.code:
b1=>box: pos (-4,0,0)
b2=>box: pos (4,0,0)
b1.move left 8 in 5 seconds async
b2.move right 8 in 3 seconds async
when b1 collides with b2 do
  sys.print "BOOM!"
end do

if-else.caption: SceneMax example for If / Else.

loops.caption: SceneMax example for Loops.
loops.code:
d => box

do 4 times
  d.turn left 90 in 0.4 seconds
end do

for-each.caption: SceneMax example for For Each.

go-conditions.caption: SceneMax example for Go Conditions.
go-conditions.code:
var game_status = 0
var ko = 1

[game_status==0 && ko==1]
game_over = {
  header.print "game over"
}

run game_over

continuous-go-conditions-go-conditions.caption: SceneMax example for Continuous Go Conditions.
continuous-go-conditions-go-conditions.code:
var player_is_jumping = 0
player1 => sinbad

#[player_is_jumping==0]
do 3 times
  player1.move left 2 in 1 second
end do

when-conditions-go-conditions.caption: SceneMax example for When Conditions.
when-conditions-go-conditions.code:
var door_open = 0

when door_open==1 do
  header.print "door opened"
end do

door_open = 1

quick-start-2.caption: Declare an imported effect and play it at the player model.
quick-start-2.code:
player => sinbad
fire_fx => effects.effekseer.A_Salamander1
fire_fx.play pos (player)

declaring-an-effekseer-effect.caption: Declare a real imported effect from the fighting game project.

playing-an-effect.caption: Play a ring effect in front of the camera.

play-at-a-fixed-position-playing-an-effe.caption: Play an effect at a specific point in the world.

play-at-another-entity-playing-an-effect.caption: Play a hit flash at the player model.
play-at-another-entity-playing-an-effect.code:
player => sinbad
hit_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Sprite_BillBoard
hit_fx.play pos (player)

start-from-a-predefined-placement-playin.caption: Declare an effect with initial placement, then play it at the enemy.
start-from-a-predefined-placement-playin.code:
enemy => ninja : pos (0,0,8)
fire_fx => effects.effekseer.A_Salamander1 : pos (0,1,0) and rotate (0,45,0)
fire_fx.play pos (enemy)

runtime-attributes.caption: Play the same effect with a faster playback speed.
runtime-attributes.code:
player => sinbad
burst_fx => effects.effekseer.A_Salamander1
burst_fx.play pos (player), attr = ["play_back_speed" 2]

looping-an-effect.caption: Create a looped aura around a player.
looping-an-effect.code:
player => sinbad
aura_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
aura_fx.play pos (player), loop

example-multiple-runtime-inputs-looping-.caption: Loop a ring effect and pass speed plus input channels.
example-multiple-runtime-inputs-looping-.code:
player => sinbad
aura_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
aura_fx.play pos (player), loop, attr = [
  "play_back_speed" 0.8,
  "input0" 1,
  "input1" 0.25
]

aiming-the-effect-before-play.caption: Aim a laser effect at an enemy before playing it.
aiming-the-effect-before-play.code:
player => sinbad
enemy => ninja : pos (0,0,8)
beam_fx => effects.effekseer.Laser01
beam_fx.look at (enemy)
beam_fx.play pos (player)

face-another-object-aiming-the-effect-be.caption: Aim a laser effect from the player toward the enemy.
face-another-object-aiming-the-effect-be.code:
player => sinbad
enemy => ninja : pos (0,0,8)
beam_fx => effects.effekseer.Laser01
beam_fx.look at (enemy)
beam_fx.play pos (player)

load-with-initial-rotation-aiming-the-ef.caption: Create a laser effect with an initial rotation.

attaching-effects-to-other-game-objects.caption: Attach an aura to the player and play it.
attaching-effects-to-other-game-objects.code:
player => sinbad
aura_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
aura_fx.attach to player
aura_fx.play pos (player)

attach-to-a-model-attaching-effects-to-o.caption: Attach a ring aura to the player model.
attach-to-a-model-attaching-effects-to-o.code:
player => sinbad
aura_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
aura_fx.attach to player
aura_fx.play pos (player)

attach-to-a-joint-attaching-effects-to-o.caption: Attach a spark effect to fighter1's right hand joint.
attach-to-a-joint-attaching-effects-to-o.code:
player => dynamic fighter1 : pos (0,0,0), scale 3,
  joints ("mixamorig:RightHand") async
hand_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Sprite_BillBoard
hand_fx.attach to player."mixamorig:RightHand"
hand_fx.play pos (player."mixamorig:RightHand")

attach-with-offset-attaching-effects-to-.caption: Attach a ring effect above the player with an offset.
attach-with-offset-attaching-effects-to-.code:
player => sinbad
aura_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
aura_fx.attach to player : pos (0,1,0)
aura_fx.play pos (player)

detach-from-parent-attaching-effects-to-.caption: Attach an effect, play it, then detach it from the player.
detach-from-parent-attaching-effects-to-.code:
player => sinbad
aura_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
aura_fx.attach to player
aura_fx.play pos (player), loop
wait 1 seconds
aura_fx.detach from parent

show-hide-and-delete.caption: Hide, show, and delete a looped effect.
show-hide-and-delete.code:
player => sinbad
portal_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
portal_fx.play pos (player), loop
portal_fx.hide
portal_fx.show
portal_fx.delete

hide-show-hide-and-delete.caption: Create a looped effect, then hide it without destroying it.
hide-show-hide-and-delete.code:
player => sinbad
portal_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
portal_fx.play pos (player), loop
portal_fx.hide

show-show-hide-and-delete.caption: Hide a looped effect, then show it again.
show-show-hide-and-delete.code:
player => sinbad
portal_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
portal_fx.play pos (player), loop
portal_fx.hide
portal_fx.show

delete-show-hide-and-delete.caption: Create an effect and delete it after it is no longer needed.
delete-show-hide-and-delete.code:
player => sinbad
portal_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
portal_fx.play pos (player), loop
portal_fx.delete

full-example.caption: A compact combat effect flow using real imported effects.
full-example.code:
player => sinbad
enemy => ninja : pos (0,0,8)

shield_fx => effects.effekseer.Simple_Ring_Shape1
beam_fx => effects.effekseer.Laser01

shield_fx.attach to player
shield_fx.play pos (player), loop

beam_fx.look at (enemy)
beam_fx.play pos (player), attr = ["play_back_speed" 1.5]

wait 1 seconds
shield_fx.hide

wait 1 seconds
shield_fx.show

wait 1 seconds
shield_fx.delete
beam_fx.delete

packaging-and-scene-changes.caption: Declare the effect so SceneMax knows to include it in the package.

why-a-new-path-is-required.caption: SceneMax example from effekseer-particle-designer.md.

keyboard-events.caption: SceneMax example for Keyboard Events.
keyboard-events.code:
player1 => sinbad

when key up is pressed once do
  player1.move forward 3 in 1 second
end do

mouse-events.caption: SceneMax example for Mouse Events.
mouse-events.code:
player => sinbad

when mouse left is pressed on player do
  player.turn left 360 in 1 second
end do

groups-and-ray-checks.caption: SceneMax example for Groups & Ray Checks.
groups-and-ray-checks.code:
player => sinbad
enemy1 => box
enemy1 belongs to the enemies group

if enemies.ray check from (player) do
  header.print "enemy in sight"
end do

designer-workflow.caption: Fighting-game-style example for Designer Workflow.
designer-workflow.code:
player1.ik = "ik_test_ik"
player1.ik.layer2.play : target player2.weapon.colliders["weapon_sphere_collider_1"], blend 0.2, weight 1
wait 0.5 seconds
player1.ik = empty

project-settings.caption: Fighting-game-style example for Project Settings.
project-settings.code:
using fighter1,fighter2 model
using punch1,kick3 audio
using perfect1 sprite

header.print "Build ready for itch.io upload"

quick-example.caption: SceneMax example from lighting.md.
quick-example.code:
sun => Lights.directional : color "#fff3d2", intensity 3.0, direction (-0.3,-0.8,-0.4), shadow high
lamp => Lights.point : pos (2,4,1), color warm, intensity 900 lumens, range 12, shadow medium
stageSpot => Lights.spot : pos (0,6,-4), look at player1, angle 35, intensity 2500, shadow on
environment => Lights.sky : preset "Night Neon", exposure 0.2, ambient "#223344"

light-types-in-detail.caption: SceneMax example from lighting.md.
light-types-in-detail.code: sun => Lights.directional : color "#fff3d2", intensity 3.0, direction (-0.3,-0.8,-0.4), shadow high

designer-workflow-2.caption: SceneMax example from lighting.md.
designer-workflow-2.code: keyLight => Lights.spot : pos (-3,5,-4), direction (0.4,-0.8,0.3), color warm, intensity 1800, range 25, angle 30, shadow medium

how-to-read-this-reference.caption: Fighting-game-style example for How To Read This Reference.
how-to-read-this-reference.code:
// After an AI tool adds a primitive or UI widget, validate it in game code
debug.on
run set_camera_on_player
header.print "Check generated scene entities"

plugin-registration.caption: SceneMax example from meshy-ai-plugin.md.

motion-easing.caption: SceneMax example from movement-and-rotation.md.
motion-easing.code:
player.move forward 4 in 1.5 seconds ease in
player.move forward 4 for 1.5 seconds ease out
player.rotate(y 90) in 0.6 seconds ease in out
player.rotate to (y 180) in 0.8 seconds ease out

functions-motion-easing.caption: SceneMax example from movement-and-rotation.md.
functions-motion-easing.code: player.move to (1,2,3) in 3 seconds ease in out "Cubic" (0.25)

s => sinbad
s.replay points in 10 seconds

procedures-with-arguments.caption: SceneMax example for Procedures with Arguments.
procedures-with-arguments.code:
s => sinbad
n => ninja

spin_model(m) = {
  m.turn 360 in 1 second
}

run spin_model(s)
run spin_model(n)

returning-values.caption: SceneMax example for Returning Values.
returning-values.code:
var score_bonus = 0

get_bonus = {
  score_bonus = 25
}

var score = 100
run get_bonus
score = score + score_bonus

creating-a-throw-motion-asset.caption: SceneMax example from throw-motion.md.
creating-a-throw-motion-asset.code:
motion = system.motion("motion_axe_throw", target enemy)
motion.apply axe async

wait.caption: SceneMax example for Wait.
wait.code:
header.print "Door opens in 2 seconds"
wait 2 seconds
header.print "Door opened"

distance.caption: SceneMax example for Distance.

angle.caption: SceneMax example for Angle.
angle.code:

enemy => ninja : pos (5,0,5)

random-numbers.code:

wait 2 seconds

predefined-shortcut-expressions.caption: SceneMax example for Predefined Shortcut Expressions.
predefined-shortcut-expressions.code:
var coll = 0
var is_jumping = 0
var ko = 0
var @allow_attack = coll==0 && is_jumping==0 && ko==0

if @allow_attack {
  header.print "attack allowed"
}

designer-workflow-3.caption: Fighting-game-style example for Designer Workflow.
designer-workflow-3.code:

player1.weapon = "weapon_player_weapon"

player1."Sword Slash"
